title: Eragrostis glomerata
taxonomy: Eragrostis glomerata is a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Chloridoideae and is further classified into the tribe Cynodonteae. The genus Eragrostis is part of the subtribe Eragrostidinae. This classification helps identify the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics. Eragrostis glomerata's classification is essential for understanding its biology and ecological role.
uses: Eragrostis glomerata has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. It is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce swelling and pain in the body. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective against certain types of infections. In addition, Eragrostis glomerata is thought to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the body against free radicals and oxidative stress. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Eragrostis glomerata for medicinal purposes.

parts: Eragrostis glomerata, also known as the woolly veldtgrass, is a plant species native to the Southern Hemisphere.
